title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
During an interrogation, Nancy Duane refuses to discuss the deaths of a priest, his wife, and Jennie Hobbs from Gleeholm, but she asks Poirot about the deaths of Harriet, Edith, and Richard, whom Poirot confirms were poisoned. Nancy mentions her late father, Algernon Johnson, and notes she keeps an amateur painting of him because he is smiling in it.

Inspector Stanley Bill interrupts to inform Poirot that two keys, engraved with Bluxton Hotel room numbers 121 and 317, were found in the pocket of a blue fur-collared coat belonging to Mrs. Dunn. Poirot reveals that two murders occurred in those specific rooms. He further notes that a witness saw Nancy dropping keys numbered 101 and 301 near the Blaxland Hotel on the night of the crime. Nancy claims she is being framed by someone who dislikes Harriet, Ada, and Richard. Poirot decides not to arrest her immediately, reasoning that it would be illogical for a killer to keep such incriminating keys.

Poirot travels to the Wallace household to speak with Louisa Wallace, observing the chaotic state of the house and the clumsiness of their new maid, Dorcas. Louisa shows Poirot a portrait painted by Nancy. Regarding the night of the crime, Louisa provides an alibi for Nancy, stating that Nancy arrived at six o'clock to deliver the painting and stayed until approximately ten o'clock. Louisa asserts that at 8:30 p.m., she, Nancy, and Saint John were all sitting together at the table.
